threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch at the sound of the bus brakes outside your window. Locals have mastered the art of sleeping through it.
Heard a bloke on the N37 loudly arguing with a pigeon about whether it flew or walked. The pigeon looked indifferent, classic South London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One morning, I spotted a guy playing bagpipes in front of a kebab shop while people casually walked by, like it was an everyday symphony.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings. It's like the city's pulse beats different here, unpredictable.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by their reaction to a BART delay; it’s just another Tuesday, while newcomers are ready to freak out.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling three apples while waiting for the 31. The bus rolled by, and he just kept juggling—never breaking focus. Classic Tenderloin moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals eat paella for lunch. Most of us go for a 'bocadillo' and a 'cervesa' at the bar instead. Much simpler.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by the way they insist on ordering paella for lunch, completely unaware it’s a dinner dish here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'excuse me' on the bus, like it's a shop queue. Around here, it's an elbow-first affair.
Last Saturday on the 133, a bloke started singing '90s R&B, but only the choruses. Everyone joined in at the stops, it was like karaoke on wheels.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using an old mattress as a makeshift trampoline in the park, I knew this place had a spirit of its own.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar that serves cocktails in old soda bottles—it's like drinking history with a twist.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know how to dodge the BART delays and still make it to a taco truck before the lunch rush hits. Tourists? They’re still stuck at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the 31 Balboa at 5 PM, passengers just staring, phones out. It’s like a living art gallery—totally San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll into any bar and get a proper brunch. Here, brunch is a more civilized affair, often starting after 11 am.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their bewilderment at the confusing metro map—especially when they’re staring at the signs, trying to make sense of "L1" and "L3".
